summary: electron-orbit captures process.env at postinstall and exfiltrates it via custom TLS to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net
details: On `npm install`, install.js (declared as the postinstall script in package.json) serialises process.env (filtering out keys containing 'path') and writes it to `electron_orbit_install_marker.txt` in the OS temp directory, alongside an inert decoy file carrying ELF/MZ/MachO magic bytes plus JSON to masquerade as a native bootstrap artifact. On first `require()` of the module, dist/index.js executes a top-level setupAutoRenderOnImport()/prefetchRegisteredIcons() flow that reads the marker file, XOR-encodes its contents with the literal key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes the result, appends it to a URL, and transmits it to `electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net`. The destination URL, the strings 'process', 'env', 'node:net', 'connect', the marker filename, and the HTTP request line are all hidden behind an XOR string-decoder routine `__s(key, data[])`. Network delivery is performed by a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (ClientHello, ECDH P-256, HKDF, AES-128-GCM record layer) speaking directly over a raw `node:net` socket to port 443, bypassing node:https and any standard proxy/agent or TLS instrumentation. URL construction is gated by a check that sha256(process.env.BuildType) is a substring of a hardcoded 64-char hex constant, so the exfil activates only on attacker-selected build environments (specific CI pipelines) and stays dormant on developer laptops or scanners that lack the matching BuildType. The package name and author metadata ('electron-orbit', 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage referencing orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) impersonate unrelated Electron/OrbitDB projects, and the README presents the package as a benign native bootstrap installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
